title: Unintentional Contemplative Practice
definition: The spontaneous experience of a state similar to meditation, achieved unintentionally through focusing on a repetitive visual pattern.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Staring at a repeating pattern like tiles causes the mind to wander and enter a meditative-like state. This is not just daydreaming; it's a unique mental event.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The blurring of visual stimuli due to sensory adaptation leads to a diffuse attention mode, allowing for creative thought, mindfulness, and even moments of pure consciousness without a self-aware subject.
difficulty_levels.expert: Unintentional Contemplative Practice is the convergence of physical tuning out (neurological event) with conscious letting go (halting the 'self' that does the interpreting). It offers a brief glimpse into the silent ground of awareness, a state sought in many contemplative traditions.

extra_fields.insight: This momentary experience can be seen as an unintentional taste of a core goal in meditation: mindfulness and understanding the nature of reality without thought or self-aware subject.
